mcp.page_title: MCP · Connect Shoehorn to your IDE
mcp.hero_subtitle: Shoehorn speaks the Model Context Protocol. Generate a read-only token, paste the config into Claude Desktop, Cursor, or any MCP-capable IDE, and start asking questions about your catalog from where you actually write code.
mcp.step1_description: Creates a Personal Access Token scoped to mcp:read. It can call every read-only MCP tool and nothing else. You'll see the raw token once; copy it immediately.
mcp.step1_token_warning: Shown only once. If you lose it, revoke it from your profile security tab and generate a new one.
mcp.step1_error_generic: Couldn't generate a token. Try again.
mcp.step2_description: Copy the snippet for your client. The token from Step 1 is already filled in. Paste the whole block into the indicated config file, restart the client, and you're connected.
mcp.step2_token_placeholder_notice: Generate a token above and it will appear in these snippets automatically.
mcp.step2_claude_desktop_path:
Config file path:
  macOS    ~/Library/Application Support/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json
  Windows  %APPDATA%\Claude\claude_desktop_config.json
  Linux    ~/.config/claude/claude_desktop_config.json
mcp.step2_vscode_path: File: .vscode/mcp.json (workspace), or User Settings → MCP: Servers
mcp.step2_copilot_cli_path: Run in a terminal where the Copilot CLI is installed.
mcp.step2_claude_web_path: claude.ai → Settings → Connectors → Add custom server
mcp.step2_mcp_remote_path: For IDEs that don't yet support remote MCP, proxy via the mcp-remote npm package.
mcp.step2_restart_note: Restart your client after saving the config file.
mcp.step3_description: In your IDE chat, ask a tool-aware model to run the whoami tool. You should see your identity, tenant, and scopes echoed back.
mcp.step3_suggested_prompt: Call the Shoehorn MCP whoami tool and tell me who I am.
mcp.step3_curl_hint: Or verify from the terminal with curl:
mcp.security_note_scope: The token carries only mcp:read. It can't modify anything in Shoehorn.
mcp.security_note_revoke: Revoke tokens any time under Profile → Security → Personal Access Tokens.
mcp.security_note_audit: Every MCP tool call is audit-logged with your user and token ID.
mcp.security_note_rate: Each token is rate-limited to protect against runaway IDE loops.
